core_mechanism: Supervised estimation of a linear scoring function over categorical feature counts — learning per-element weights by regressing an aggregated score against continuous outcome labels — where predictive accuracy is governed by an aggregation/pooling trade-off between the volume of samples pooled and the amount of statistically independent training signal available at each grouping level
brief_reason: The abstract trains sentiment scores (learned lexical feature weights) against return and volatility labels at three aggregation levels and attributes the reversal in which feature set performs best to the interaction between document volume and the independent training signal available at each level of aggregation.
